THE COURT DID NOT RECEIVE COURTESY COPIES OF PLAINTIFF'S MOVING PAPERS PER LOCAL RULE 2.7(B) AND REQUIRED PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL TO PAY A $60 MOTION FILING FEE VIA E-FILING IN LIEU OF FILING A NEW MOTION. MASTER CALENDAR MOTION ON MAY-19-2022 IN DEPT. 206, PLAINTIFF NAZARET WOLDU'S MOTION FOR AN ORDER MOVING THE TRIAL AND RELATED DEADLINES BY THREE MONTHS CONTINUED FROM MAY-19-2022 TO MASTER CALENDAR MOTION AT MAY-24-2022, 9:30 AM IN DEPT. 206. TODD M. FRIEDMAN, ESQ. FOR PLAINTIFF NAZARET WOLDU VIA COURTCALL. NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT TENTATIVELY GRANTED THE MOTION TO CONTINUE THE TRIAL DATE, HOWEVER ONLY TO A DATE IN SEPTEMBER AND NOT TO THE PROPOSED DATE IN NOVEMBER. COUNSEL ORDERED TO MEET AND CONFER RE A NEW TRIAL DATE IN SEPTEMBER AND THE COURT CONTINUED THE MOTION TO 05/24/22 AT 9:30AM IN DEPT. 206 FOR COUNSEL TO PROVIDE AN AGREEABLE SEPTEMBER DATE. PLAINTIFF TO GIVE NOTICE OF THE CONTINUED MOTION DATE OF 05/24/22. IN ADDITION, THE COURT DID NOT RECEIVE COURTESY COPIES OF PLAINTIFF'S MOVING PAPERS PER LOCAL RULE 2.7(B) AND WILL REQUIRE PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL TO PAY A $60 MOTION FILING FEE IN LIEU OF FILING A NEW MOTION. Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File A Second Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Oct-27-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF NAZARET WOLDU's Motion For Leave To File A Second Amended Complaint.

No appearances are made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Plaintiff's motion for leave to file a second amended complaint is granted. The motion is supported by the strong policy of liberality in permitting amendments to pleadings. Defendant has not shown that plaintiff unduly delayed in seeking leave to amend nor that it would be prejudiced by the proposed amendment, particularly because defendant has been on notice since February 2021 that plaintiff intended to add the specified claims. The Court also notes that plaintiff has moved for a continuance of the trial date, which would ensure that defendant has ample time to challenge the pleadings and complete discovery.

Order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported (302/EPS)
